title: If 1
description: Rust uses if/else expressions for conditional logic. The condition must be a boolean.
instructions: Complete the function to return the correct string based on the number.
hints: Use an if/else expression to compare a and b.
Remember that if/else in Rust is an expression that returns a value.
You can write: if a > b { a } else { b }
explanation: The `if` expression in Rust evaluates a boolean condition and executes the corresponding block. Unlike many languages, `if` in Rust is an expression, meaning it returns a value. Both branches must return the same type.
